notes: 설비 레이어의 ARC/CIRCLE 위주 시설물 심볼; 벽 기하 없음.

rationale.evidence: All 32 entities sit on layer A06-시설물-설비 with a histogram dominated by ARC=21 plus CIRCLE=3 and only 8 short LINE segments. Sampled lines are short orthogonal stubs (e.g. 1E0B, 1E10–1E13, 1E15) and circles have fixture-scale radii (5.3–17.9), not wall-length runs. The mix matches a plumbing/MEP fixture glyph rather than room or wall drafting.
rationale.rule: When a block lives entirely on a facility/MEP equipment layer and is composed mostly of arcs/circles with short connector lines, classify it as a symbol and assign near-zero wall likelihood.
rationale.counterfactual: If long paired parallel lines on a wall/structure layer appeared at a constant ~100–200 unit offset, or if the layer were WAL/A-WALL instead of 시설물-설비, the judgment would flip toward 평면 부분도 with populated wall_line_handles.
rationale.uncertainty: ARC centers/radii and circle centers are not fully projected, so the exact fixture type (drain, valve, outlet) cannot be named; insertion context in the host floor plan is also unknown.

notes: 수전 레이어 ARC 군집의 수전/급수 기구 심볼.

rationale.evidence: Every entity is on layer 수전; ARC=85 dominates with only small CIRCLE radii (2.5) and short LINE segments such as 68A1–68A6 (~3–4 units) and diagonal stubs 68A7–68AA. The ~90×90 bbox and faucet-named layer indicate a water-fixture symbol, not architectural wallwork.
rationale.rule: A block wholly on a faucet/fixture layer whose geometry is dense arcs plus tiny connector lines is a fixture symbol; do not treat those stubs as walls.
rationale.counterfactual: Presence of long double-line wall pairs on a wall layer, or renaming/relayering onto WAL with continuous room-scale edges, would raise wall_likelihood and change the role toward 평면 부분도.
rationale.uncertainty: Individual ARC parameters are unsampled, so brand/model of the faucet and whether it includes a basin outline cannot be confirmed from this projection alone.

notes: FUR 레이어 대형 수납/가구 입면형 평면 디테일.

rationale.evidence: All 144 entities are on FUR with many parallel horizontal LINEs (621A, 6220, 6222, 6224–6225, 6230–6237) spanning ~111 units and verticals 6221/6223/6226/6227 forming a cabinet box. ARC/ELLIPSE/SPLINE counts add decorative or hardware detail typical of furniture, not wall cores. Bbox spans hundreds of units consistent with a vanity/cabinet assembly.
rationale.rule: Closed or stacked parallel outlines confined to a FUR furniture layer are furniture geometry even when outer edges look rectilinear; wall_line_handles stay empty unless a wall/structure layer is present.
rationale.counterfactual: If the same outer rectangle lived on _A-WAL / WALL as a thickness pair (~100–200 apart) without FUR shelving hatch lines, it would read as wall and wall_line_handles would list those edges.
rationale.uncertainty: Whether this is a wall-hung vanity versus freestanding cabinet, and which face is the wall side, cannot be told without insertion context; nested use on _A-WAL-2 elsewhere is outside this definition's own layers.

notes: FUR-FIX/FUR-KIT 고정·키친 가구 박스 아웃라인.

rationale.evidence: 21 entities on FUR-FIX and 3 on FUR-KIT; geometry is LINE/LWPOLYLINE rectangles and tall vertical runs (e.g. 33EF/33F0 from y=980 to 920, 33F2/33F3 from -50 to 20) forming fixed-furniture boxes. No wall-named layer appears. Diagonal 33F5/33F7 look like door-swing or corner marks on furniture, not wall faces.
rationale.rule: Blocks whose layers are explicitly FUR-FIX/FUR-KIT and whose entities are rectangular cabinet footprints should be labeled 가구 with negligible wall likelihood.
rationale.counterfactual: Relayering the long verticals onto a wall layer as a parallel thickness pair, or adding WAL inserts, would flip this toward 평면 부분도 and fill wall_line_handles.
rationale.uncertainty: Product identity (closet vs kitchen unit) and whether the ~1000-unit height is plan depth or elevation bleed cannot be confirmed from the sparse sample alone.

notes: I-FL-FUR(S) 대형 가구/붙박이 윤곽; 외곽선이 벽처럼 보일 수 있음.

rationale.evidence: Entire content is on I-FL-FUR(S) with LINE=108 plus arcs; sampled edges include room-scale runs such as 4150 (0,-1480)->(0,0) and L-shaped returns 4151–4152, plus dense local fixture detailing near x≈-1020. The FUR(S) layer name marks floor furniture, but the long orthogonal perimeter can visually resemble wall faces.
rationale.rule: Furniture-layer blocks with long orthogonal outer edges get role 가구; list only those perimeter lines that could be mistaken for walls in wall_line_handles and keep wall_likelihood low-to-moderate.
rationale.counterfactual: If those long edges were duplicated as a parallel pair on a wall layer at wall thickness, or if TIL/WAL room composition inserts appeared, role would shift to 평면 부분도 and wall_likelihood would rise above 0.5.
rationale.uncertainty: Most of the 146 entities are unsampled, so interior partition vs single cabinet carcass is unclear; true wall adjacency depends on unseen host-plan insertion.

notes: FUR 대형 복합 가구(원·스플라인·프레임) 평면 심볼성 가구.

rationale.evidence: All 345 entities are on FUR; outer rectangle 6AFF–6B02 (~385×535) plus inner parallels 6B03–6B06, CIRCLE radii 32.5/23, and heavy ARC/SPLINE counts describe a richly detailed furniture piece. No wall/structure layer is present. Outer frame lines are the only candidates that look wall-like.
rationale.rule: Rich FUR-layer assemblies with decorative circles/splines inside a rectangular frame are furniture; outer frame lines may be noted as wall-lookalikes but should not drive a wall role.
rationale.counterfactual: Stripping interior furniture detail and placing a parallel thickness pair on WAL would reclassify this as wall/부분도 content.
rationale.uncertainty: Exact furniture type (bed, sofa, island) cannot be resolved because most arcs/splines lack coordinates in the sample; scale relative to the room is insertion-dependent.

notes: I-FL-FUR(B) 장축 붙박이/수납 가구와 수직 칸막이.

rationale.evidence: All entities are on I-FL-FUR(B); long horizontals 4F44 (~1820 units) and 4F4C plus many vertical dividers (4F47–4F4B, 4F5B, 4F5C) and X-marked LWPOLYLINE corners form a built-in cabinet run. Layer naming and repeated bay spacing are furniture, not wall cores.
rationale.rule: Floor-furniture (I-FL-FUR) blocks with long runs and regular vertical partitions are 가구; only the extreme long edges warrant low-confidence wall-lookalike listing.
rationale.counterfactual: If the long horizontals were a parallel pair on a wall layer ~150 apart without cabinet dividers, they would be true wall faces and role would become 평면 부분도.
rationale.uncertainty: Whether this is a wardrobe, kitchen bank, or vanity run, and which side abuts a wall, cannot be determined without the host insert and unsampled polylines.

notes: 타일 그리드+가구 INSERT 조합의 욕실/유닛 평면 부분도.

rationale.evidence: The block mixes TIL grid LINEs at 300-unit modules (6436–643F, 644A–644E), FUR INSERTs of named products (코너 선반, IW990, T1, KH-530S-1 TOP), an MC polyline, and a FUR baseline 6446. This is a composed bathroom/unit bay rather than a single symbol or lone furniture definition. No direct WAL-layer LINE sample appears.
rationale.rule: Anonymous ba* unit blocks that compose tile grids plus multiple furniture INSERTs are 평면 부분도; tile joint lines are not walls unless on a wall layer or forming thickness pairs.
rationale.counterfactual: If continuous double-line wall outlines on _A-WAL appeared instead of TIL joints, wall_likelihood would jump and those handles would fill wall_line_handles; if only one furniture INSERT remained with no TIL/MC, role would drop to 가구/심볼.
rationale.uncertainty: Walls may exist only inside nested INSERTs not exploded here; TIL lines could coincide with wall faces in the host drawing but that cannot be proven from this projection.

notes: 가구 INSERT·TIL 그리드 욕실 유닛 부분 평면도.

rationale.evidence: Six FUR INSERTs name fixtures/furniture (코너 선반, BS-03 TOP, KH-530S-1 TOP, SB1703 1500, IC708, 세면대_공통) while TIL lines form a 300-module grid (58D3–58DF) and MC supplies a boundary polyline. Entity mix is a composed wet-area partial plan, not a solitary symbol.
rationale.rule: Unit blocks combining multiple product INSERTs with tile-grid lines belong to 평면 부분도; classify TIL orthogonals as finish joints, not walls, absent wall-layer evidence.
rationale.counterfactual: Removing the TIL/MC composition and leaving a single FUR insert would make this 가구/심볼; adding explicit wall-thickness LINE pairs on WAL would raise wall_likelihood substantially.
rationale.uncertainty: No wall-layer entities are sampled, so whether the module boundary is a true wall or only tile extent remains unknown; nested block interiors are opaque.

notes: FUR 디테일+TIL+INSERT가 섞인 욕실 유닛 부분도.

rationale.evidence: Layer mix TIL+FUR+MC with INSERT 코너 선반, dense FUR detailing (arcs, SOLID hatch 64A3), and TIL grid lines 6490–6499 shows a composed bathroom bay. Parallel FUR horizontals 649A/649B at y=-900/-860 span the full 1500 width and could be read as a thin partition or wall face pair (~40 apart).
rationale.rule: Composed ba* modules with tile grids and furniture inserts are 평면 부분도; only long parallel edges that could be thickness pairs earn wall_line_handles, still at modest likelihood if on FUR not WAL.
rationale.counterfactual: If 649A/649B lived on _A-WAL with ~100–200 spacing and no furniture hatch clutter, wall_likelihood would exceed 0.7; if the block collapsed to only the tiny FUR glyph near 863,-880, role would become 심볼.
rationale.uncertainty: Whether 649A/649B are a wall, curb, or vanity backplane cannot be settled from layer FUR alone; most of 144 entities remain unsampled.

wall_line_handles.reason: long vertical edge possible wall face

notes: _A-WAL-2·욕조 INSERT와 TIL/FUR이 있는 욕실 부분 평면도.

rationale.evidence: Besides TIL grid and FUR product INSERTs, the block includes INSERT 62CA on _A-WAL-2 (nested A$C71B42895) and DS-F-BATH INSERT 62CB, signaling wall-associated and bath fixtures inside a unit plan. Vertical FUR segments 62C1/62C2/62C5 at x=450 form a broken long edge that can read as a wall face. TIL lines remain finish grid, not walls.
rationale.rule: Partial-plan unit blocks that explicitly INSERT content on a wall-named layer plus room finishes/fixtures are 평면 부분도 with elevated wall_likelihood even when many wall strokes are nested.
rationale.counterfactual: Absent the _A-WAL-2 and DS-F-BATH inserts and the x=450 edge, this would look like a pure tile+furniture collage with wall_likelihood near 0.1; exploded wall thickness pairs would push likelihood higher still.
rationale.uncertainty: Wall geometry inside INSERT 62CA is not visible here, so actual wall handles inside the nest are unknown; gaps in 62C1–62C5 may be openings rather than continuous wall.

notes: 소형 타일 그리드+가구 INSERT 욕실 유닛 부분도.

rationale.evidence: Only 14 entities: MC boundary, four FUR INSERTs (IC708, 코너 선반, BS-03 TOP, SB1703 1500), and TIL grid LINEs at 300/600 modules (645E–6466). Pattern matches other ba* wet-unit partial plans without a sampled wall layer.
rationale.rule: Small composed blocks of tile-grid lines plus multiple furniture INSERTs remain 평면 부분도; lack of wall-layer geometry keeps wall_likelihood low and wall_line_handles empty.
rationale.counterfactual: Adding _A-WAL inserts or parallel wall-thickness lines would raise wall_likelihood; reducing to a single INSERT with no TIL would reclassify as 가구/심볼.
rationale.uncertainty: Walls may be implied only by host context or nested blocks; TIL extents versus true room boundary cannot be distinguished from this thin projection.

notes: _A-WAL-2·욕조 INSERT 포함 욕실 유닛 부분 평면도.

rationale.evidence: Same composition family as ba17001400: TIL grid, FUR product INSERTs, MC polyline, DS-F-BATH INSERT 647A, and _A-WAL-2 INSERT 6476 of A$C71B42895. FUR horizontals 6477–6479 at y=450 form a broken full-width edge that can act as a wall face. This is a bathroom unit partial plan with explicit wall-layer nesting.
rationale.rule: When a unit block both composes finishes/fixtures and INSERTs on a wall-named layer, role is 평면 부분도 and wall_likelihood should be moderately elevated; list only host-level edges that look like wall faces.
rationale.counterfactual: Without _A-WAL-2/DS-F-BATH and without the y=450 edge chain, wall_likelihood would fall to ~0.1; visible exploded wall pairs on WAL would push it above 0.6.
rationale.uncertainty: Nested wall drawing inside 6476 is opaque; whether 6477–6479 are wall, curb, or furniture back edge remains ambiguous on FUR.

notes: BS-03 TOP 선반/수납 가구 평면; FUR LINE만 존재.

rationale.evidence: Name BS-03 TOP and all 33 LINEs on FUR show a rectangular shelf/cabinet with repeated horizontal slats (1D8C–1D97) between vertical sides 1D7C/1D81. Coordinates are large world-space values typical of product blocks, with no wall layer or thickness pair.
rationale.rule: Named product TOP blocks composed solely of FUR rectangular/slat linework are 가구 (furniture symbols in plan), not walls or partial plans.
rationale.counterfactual: If the outer pair were on WAL at wall thickness without slat fills, or if the block aggregated room TIL/INSERTs, classification would change to wall/부분도.
rationale.uncertainty: Real width/depth after insertion scale is unknown from absolute world coordinates; whether BS-03 is wall-mounted cannot be seen without the host plan.

notes: I-FL-FUR(B) 수납장 박스와 X형 코너 마크.

rationale.evidence: Almost all entities are on I-FL-FUR(B): outer box 3882–3886/3892, shelf horizontals 3885/3889/388A, and X-crossing lines 388F/3890 and 3894/3895 typical of cabinet door/end marks, plus a lone _0 vertical 389E. This is built-in furniture drafting, not a room partial plan.
rationale.rule: I-FL-FUR cabinet boxes with X end-marks are 가구; long sides may be listed as wall-lookalikes but stay low likelihood without a wall layer.
rationale.counterfactual: Parallel copies of 3882/3883 on a wall layer at ~150 offset without shelf lines would indicate a true wall pair and raise wall_likelihood sharply.
rationale.uncertainty: ARC details 3899/389B/389D are unsampled so hardware type is unclear; wall adjacency of the cabinet is insertion-dependent.

notes: WID 레이어 cw1200 창호(창) 심볼; 양단 벽후 표현.

rationale.evidence: Name cw1200 and all entities on WID span exactly width 1200 with depth 100; end pockets 0–50 and 1150–1200 (7A1C/7A09 plus returns 7A1E–7A1F/7A06–7A07) depict wall thickness at jambs, while 7A0A/7A0B mark the glazing centerline. This is a window opening symbol that intentionally includes short wall stubs.
rationale.rule: WID-layer cw* blocks sized to a nominal clear width with 50–100 deep jamb rectangles are window symbols; jamb segments count as wall-line lookalikes and justify mid wall_likelihood without changing role to 평면 부분도.
rationale.counterfactual: If the block contained full room walls, furniture INSERTs, or tile grids beyond the 1200 opening, it would become 평면 부분도; if jamb returns were omitted and only glass lines remained, wall_likelihood would drop near 0.
rationale.uncertainty: Whether cw means curtain-wall or casement window, and the true host wall thickness, cannot be verified because only the symbolized stub length is shown.

notes: WID 레이어 CW900 창호 심볼; 900폭 개구+벽후 잼.

rationale.evidence: Identical WID window pattern to cw1200 but scaled to width 900: jamb pockets at 0–50 and 850–900 (79A9/7996 and returns), dual glazing lines 7997/7998, and head/sill 79AD/79A6. Entity histogram LINE=27 LWPOLYLINE=4 matches the sibling window block.
rationale.rule: Nominal-width CW*/cw* blocks on WID with jamb thickness stubs are window symbols; report jamb edges in wall_line_handles and keep mid wall_likelihood.
rationale.counterfactual: Extension into a multi-bay curtain-wall assembly with mullion grids across a room, or addition of wall-layer runs beyond the opening, would shift toward 평면 부분도 and higher wall_likelihood.
rationale.uncertainty: Host wall thickness may differ from the drawn 100-unit depth; swing/fixed orientation is not encoded in the sampled lines.

notes: WID dw1200 문 심볼; 문짝 LWPOLYLINE+개구선.

rationale.evidence: Name dw1200 on WID with LINE=27 and LWPOLYLINE=26; top LINE 57C8 spans the 1200 opening at y=0 while paired leaf polylines/lines at y≈-85 to -195 (57CB–57E5) describe door leaves/panels. This is a door symbol in plan, with the y=0 line representing the wall face/opening, not a full wall network.
rationale.rule: WID dw* blocks whose geometry is an opening-width header line plus door-leaf polylines are door symbols; count the wall-face opening line as a wall lookalike with moderate likelihood.
rationale.counterfactual: If multiple connected wall segments enclosing a room appeared, role would become 평면 부분도; if the y=0 line vanished and only floating leaf geometry remained, wall_likelihood would drop.
rationale.uncertainty: Swing direction, frame thickness, and whether 57C9/57CA twelve-vertex polylines are frames or leaves cannot be fully read without vertex coordinates.

notes: WID dw1400 문 심볼; 1400폭 개구+문짝.

rationale.evidence: Same WID door schema as dw1200 scaled to 1400: opening LINE 546C (1355,0)->(45,0) and mirrored leaf/panel linework (546F–5489) with many LWPOLYLINEs. Histogram matches the door family exactly. No furniture or tile composition is present.
rationale.rule: Nominal-width dw* blocks on WID combining one wall-face opening line with door-leaf polylines are 심볼; attribute moderate wall_likelihood to the opening line only.
rationale.counterfactual: Embedding this door into a larger ba*-style unit with TIL/FUR/WAL inserts would reclassify as 평면 부분도; removing 546C would leave a free-floating leaf symbol with near-zero wall_likelihood.
rationale.uncertainty: Leaf count (single vs double) and hinge side are ambiguous without polyline vertex order; true wall thickness beyond the symbolized opening is unknown.

notes: WID DW1500 문 심볼; 1500폭 개구+문짝.

rationale.evidence: WID door block DW1500 mirrors dw1200/dw1400: opening LINE 3EC6 (1455,0)->(45,0) plus leaf/panel LINEs and LWPOLYLINEs (3EC9–3EE3) sized for a 1500 clear opening. Entity mix is purely door drafting on WID with no room-composition layers.
rationale.rule: DW*/dw* WID blocks of opening line plus leaf polylines are door symbols; the opening line is the sole wall-associated handle and supports mid wall_likelihood without a 평면 부분도 role.
rationale.counterfactual: If surrounding wall-thickness pairs or unit-plan INSERTs were added, judgment would move to 평면 부분도 with higher wall_likelihood; absence of 3EC6 would collapse wall association.
rationale.uncertainty: Exact panel articulation inside the 12-vertex polylines is unseen; host wall fire-rating/thickness and swing clearances are outside this projection.
